log.reason: PMID 34755828 attached to IMpower-010 resolves on PubMed to 'Corrigendum: Human platelet lysate biotherapy for traumatic brain injury' (Brain 2021); unrelated to atezolizumab adjuvant NSCLC. Trial NCT02486718 is real IMpower-010 but pmid is wrong.

log.reason: PMID 29610497 attached to NICCO resolves to 'Use of a Laparoscopic Bag for Endoscopic Retrieval of Gastric Tumor' (Am J Gastroenterol 2018); unrelated to neoadjuvant nivolumab melanoma. NCT02434354 also requires verification.

log.reason: PMID 36648215 attached to 'IMmuNED' resolves to Cochrane Database Syst Rev 2023 'Neoadjuvant treatment for stage III and IV cutaneous melanoma' (a SR, not a primary trial). 'IMmuNED' trial name also not verifiable; possible fabricated-trial.

log.reason: PMID 18567918 attached to 'OPUS-2' (claimed 2024) resolves to 'Cilostazol on platelet function... DM/CAD' (Eur Heart J 2008); entirely unrelated and the PMID is from 2008 while trial year is 2024 — also year-pmid-era-mismatch.

log.reason: NCT identifier has invalid trailing 'b' suffix; ClinicalTrials.gov NCT IDs are NCT followed by 8 digits only. Likely fabricated or copy-paste-suffixed.

log.reason: COPE-Liver (Nasralla) row has publishedHR=-50.0 with hrLCI=-65, hrUCI=-35 and estimandType=MD, but tE/tN/cE/cN all null. An MD of -50 for a transplant graft-injury outcome is implausibly extreme and inconsistent with the published COPE-Liver primary endpoint (peak AST log-MD ~ -0.5 units). Effect-size implausibility + missing arm counts.
log.ground_truth_hint: Nasralla NEJM 2018 PMID 29670272 reports peak AST as log-transformed MD ~-0.45, not -50

log.reason: PMID 37977660 attached to PACE-A resolves to Neuron 2023 'Differential Requirement for Plexin-A3/A4 in Sensory Neurons'; unrelated to prostate SBRT. Also NCT02546050 is not the canonical PACE-A registration (PACE-A is part of NCT01584258 PACE program with sub-comparisons).

log.reason: PMID 36794895 attached to MIRAGE resolves to Acta Derm Venereol 'A Light-red Nodule on the Jaw: A Quiz'; entirely unrelated to MR-LINAC vs CBCT prostate SBRT. NCT04557098 is also flagged because it conflicts with NCT04557099 used elsewhere in the chunk (MajesTEC-1) — duplicate-adjacent-NCT pattern.

log.reason: MENSA has cE=191 and cN=191 — control event count equals control N (100% exacerbation rate), schema-incoherent (events cannot equal N for an exacerbation outcome). PMID 25199059 resolves correctly to Ortega et al NEJM 2014 MENSA (HR confirms 0.47 reduction), so this is a tE/cE/cN data corruption, not a trial-identity issue.
log.ground_truth_hint: Real MENSA reports ~290 vs 388 exacerbations in mepo vs placebo arms; cN=191 is wrong (likely value-swap)

log.reason: MIRRA EGPA row has PMID 28528561 which resolves to NEJM 2017 'Angiotensin II for Vasodilatory Shock' (ATHOS-3); completely unrelated to mepolizumab/EGPA. The NCT02596542 + trial name MIRRA + group string are otherwise plausible.

log.reason: SKYLIGHT-1 fezolinetant row has PMID 37178735 which resolves to Gastroenterology 2023 'Enterochromaffin Cells: Small in Number but Big in Impact'; unrelated to NK3R hot-flash therapy. Effect estimate (-2.39 MD) is plausible for SKYLIGHT-1.

log.reason: Row claims TANGO-I with NCT02168946; but NCT02168946 is actually TANGO-II (meropenem-vaborbactam, CRE infections). TANGO-I (cUTI) is NCT02166476. The HAP_VAP review later in this chunk reuses NCT02168946 for 'TANGO-II', confirming the swap.

log.reason: REPRISE row uses NCT01953419, but real REPRISE (ceftazidime-avibactam) is NCT01644643 (correctly listed in CARBAPENEM_RESISTANT review). NCT01953419 is an unrelated trial. PMID 27108754 also resolves to 'Stress Health' commentary, unrelated.

log.ground_truth_hint: Hill et al J Am Coll Cardiol 2020 DISRUPT-CAD-3 PMID 33049859

log.reason: MajesTEC-1 (teclistamab) row uses NCT04557099, but real MajesTEC-1 = NCT04557098. The adjacent +1 NCT digit is a classic typo/fabrication signature, and NCT04557098 is also misused as 'MIRAGE' earlier in this chunk (SBRT_PROSTATE review) — pointing to systematic NCT-recycling. Band is QUARANTINED; this is new HIGH evidence.

log.reason: MonumenTAL-1 (talquetamab) listed with NCT04634552; real MonumenTAL-1 = NCT03399799 (phase 1) / NCT04634552 is MonumenTAL-2 or a related program — verify. Already QUARANTINED, recorded as new HIGH evidence.

log.evidence: PMID 33882210 retrieved via PubMed maps to 'Platform Trials - Beware the Noncomparable Control Group' (NEJM Perspective/Editorial), NOT the TRAAP2 trial primary publication. TRAAP2 primary is Sentilhes 2021 NEJM (PMID 33882217). Likely off-by-7 PMID transcription error.
log.action: Replace with correct TRAAP2 primary publication PMID; verify N/event counts against correct source.

log.evidence: PMID 31866247 retrieved via PubMed is titled 'Advances in regional paleopathology of the Southern Coast of the Central Andes' — completely unrelated to NOBLE PCI vs CABG trial. NOBLE 5y primary is Holm et al. 2020 Lancet (PMID 31810810 area).
log.action: Replace PMID with correct NOBLE 5-year primary; re-verify HR 1.58 (1.24-2.01).

log.evidence: PMID 32614388 maps to 'Structure-based identification of small molecules against influenza A virus endonuclease' — totally unrelated to BEST DM 5y PCI/CABG.
log.action: Find and substitute correct BEST 5y primary publication PMID.

log.evidence: PMID 37874820 maps to 'Associations between smokers' knowledge of causes of smoking harm…' — no relation to LASTE thrombectomy trial. LASTE primary is Costalat et al. 2024 NEJM.

PMID 32902312 is the IMSTAR-HN protocol paper (Zech 2020 Future Oncology). The cited NCT03700905 corresponds to IMSTAR-HN, NOT to a study named 'NIVOPOSTOP'. The trial-name vs NCT-vs-PMID triplet is incoherent.
log.action: Determine whether intended record is IMSTAR-HN (use correct readout PMID when published) OR the actual NIVOPOSTOP trial NCT04671667 (Bourhis 2024). Currently extraction conflates two distinct nivolumab HNSCC trials. Year=2024 readout HR=0.66 is consistent with NIVOPOSTOP readout, so NCT field is likely wrong.

log.evidence: PMID 32135274 is titled 'Transradial access through the anatomical snuffbox: Results of a feasibility study' (cardiology procedure). Unrelated to EAGLE durvalumab HNSCC trial (Ferris 2020 Ann Oncol).
log.action: Replace PMID. Additionally, schema flag: tE=240 with tN=240 implies 100% event rate in the durvalumab arm, biologically implausible for a 2L OS analysis and inconsistent with published EAGLE OS curves — re-verify tE.

log.evidence: PMID 37137068 is titled simply 'Clinical Cases.' — does not match MAESTRO-NAFLD-1 resmetirom safety study (Harrison 2023 Nat Med).

PMID 40960239 (NEJM, 'Orforglipron, an Oral Small-Molecule GLP-1 Receptor Agonist for Obesity Treatment') matches the Orforglipron obesity readout (ATTAIN-1) and is internally consistent — KEEP. Flag retained only to note that the group field labels ATTAIN-1 as obesity but ATTAIN-2 (NCT05726136) as 'Orforglipron T2DM + obesity' (PMID 41398455 'Orforglipron: A Novel Oral GLP-1 Agonist for the Treatment of Obesity and Diabetes' is a review article, not a trial primary). ATTAIN-2 PMID is a narrative review, NOT the primary publication.
log.action: ATTAIN-1: confirm and KEEP. ATTAIN-2: replace PMID 41398455 (review article) with correct ATTAIN-2 primary readout PMID. Downgrade ATTAIN-2 finding only — split below.

log.evidence: PMID 41398455 in PubMed is 'Orforglipron: A Novel Oral GLP-1 Agonist for the Treatment of Obesity and Diabetes' published in 'Cardiology in review' — a narrative review article, not the ATTAIN-2 primary RCT publication. Extracted tE/tN data cannot be sourced from a review.
log.action: Replace with correct ATTAIN-2 primary RCT PMID once published; mark current row pre-readout placeholder.

log.evidence: CHAMPION-NMOSD has cE=20/cN=47 (placebo arm). PREVENT (NCT01892345) cited above also reports cE=20/cN=47. CHAMPION-NMOSD is a single-arm ravulizumab study that used PREVENT placebo as historical external control — the duplicate row is methodologically driven, but unflagged in extraction. Without an external-comparator flag this is a meta-analysis double-counting risk.
log.action: Tag CHAMPION-NMOSD comparator as 'historical external (PREVENT)' so any pooled analysis avoids double-counting the 20/47 placebo arm.

log.evidence: PMID 31368490 is 'Sigmoid volvulus and coffee bean sign' (case report/radiology) — unrelated to RAINBOW ranibizumab ROP RCT (Stahl 2019 Lancet).
PMID 37257872 is 'Task-Oriented Nursing Care Through a Positive Lens' — unrelated to OCS-Heart DCD vs DBD heart transplant trial (Schroder 2023 NEJM PMID 37224197).
log.action: Replace PMID with the correct Schroder 2023 NEJM primary.

log.evidence: PMID 29275914 maps to 'Downstream Imaging Utilization After Emergency Department Ultrasound' (radiology, ED). FAITH primary is Lancet 2017 (PMID 28223031).

tE=50, tN=79, cE=0, cN=0, publishedHR=null. PEDFIC-2 is open-label extension (single arm) — having cE=0/cN=0 is consistent with single-arm but should carry an explicit flag. Currently schema-clean but downstream pooling code could divide by zero on cE/cN.
log.action: Add single_arm=true; ensure downstream pipeline skips comparator math.

log.detail: KEYNOTE-522 pembrolizumab+chemo in TNBC neoadjuvant produced a *beneficial* effect on pCR and EFS (HR/RR favouring pembrolizumab); published EFS HR ~0.63. Extraction records publishedHR=1.23 [1.07,1.41] estimandType=RR, which would imply pembrolizumab is worse than control. Direction is inverted relative to the trial's primary outcome. Either the labels for tE/cE are swapped, or the HR/RR was inverted. tE/tN=313/591 in pembrolizumab arm vs cE/cN=173/401 in control implies RR=(313/591)/(173/401)=1.23 — i.e. the extractor computed RR with the *event* being non-pCR (failure), not pCR (response), but the published primary is pCR-favouring.

IMpassion031 atezolizumab+chemo TNBC neoadjuvant: published primary (pCR) showed atezolizumab benefit (~58% vs 41%). Extraction's publishedHR=1.40 [1.10,1.79] for tE/tN=95/165 vs cE/cN=69/168 implies RR=(95/165)/(69/168)=1.40 — same non-pCR framing as KEYNOTE-522 (F-7-001). Direction therefore looks reversed vs the published primary.

PMID 39167807 attached to KEYNOTE-B49 (pembrolizumab HR+ high-risk breast) is in fact 'Belzutifan versus Everolimus for Advanced Renal-Cell Carcinoma' (NEJM 2024, DOI 10.1056/NEJMoa2313906). According to PubMed. Wrong-disease, wrong-drug citation. Schema would pass any non-null PMID check but the citation is semantically wrong.

PMID 35190648 attached to CHIEF-HF (canagliflozin HFpEF/HFrEF) is in fact 'Tracking haplotypes to quantify genome erosion' in Nat Rev Genet 2022 (DOI 10.1038/s41576-022-00461-0), a wolf-genome research-highlight piece. According to PubMed. Wrong-domain citation; CHIEF-HF actual primary is Spertus 2022 in Nat Med. Schema-valid but semantically false PMID.

PMID 37882727 attached to RENOVATE is in fact 'Amphetamine-induced dopamine release in rat: whole-brain spatiotemporal analysis with [C]raclopride and PET' (J Cereb Blood Flow Metab 2023, DOI 10.1177/0271678X231210128). According to PubMed. Wrong-domain (rat PET pharmacology, not human HFNC/NIV trial). Schema-valid but semantically false PMID.

PMID 42093364 attached to VENTILATE (HFNC vs NIV immunocompromised, 2023) is in fact 'The Course of Rehabilitation of Ventilated and Tracheally Cannulated Patients in Neurological-Neurosurgical Early Rehabilitation' (Dtsch Arztebl Int, 2026, DOI 10.3238/arztebl.m2025.0241). According to PubMed. Wrong-domain (rehab care, not RCT). Schema-valid but semantically false PMID. Note: 'VENTILATE' acronym also unusual — flag trial identity too.

OPAL-HK and HARMONIZE rows have publishedHR= -1.01 and -0.70 respectively with estimandType='MD'. Hazard ratios cannot be negative; the value is a continuous MD on serum potassium (mEq/L). If the pooling engine treats publishedHR as log-HR or HR, exp(-1.01)=0.36 (silent corruption); if treated as raw HR, the math will fail or be nonsensical. This is a schema-vs-engine contract risk identical to the SE_FLOOR / mislabelled-estimand pattern. Two trials affected.

HOPE-B: tE=1.51, cE=4.18; GENEr8-1: tE=0.8, cE=4.8. tE/cE are integer event-count slots in this schema. Fractional values here are *rates* (ABR — annualized bleeding rate), not events. Computing RR or pooling these as counts with binomial variance will fail or silently misbehave. Same pattern in other hemophilia rows (12 vs 53; 21 vs 80 — these are counts, but HOPE-B and GENEr8-1 break the contract). This is a 'silent failure sentinel' risk if the engine doesn't type-check.

FIT-1: tE=13, cE=0 (n=25); FIT-2: tE=12, cE=0 (n=24). Published OR fields are 13.46 [0.83, 218.4] and 12.0 [0.74, 195.4] respectively. Wide CIs are consistent with zero-cell scenarios. Per Cochrane guidance, 0.5 continuity correction needed for log-OR variance. Engine must detect zero-cell and apply correction; otherwise variance underestimated. Schema does not carry a 'correction_applied' flag — risk of silent miscoding.

Review consists of 4 single-arm trials (Nadofaragene firadenovec, Cretostimogene CG0070, Oportuzumab monatox, Pembrolizumab KEYNOTE-057). All have cE=cN=0 and null HR — encoded correctly, but a review of 4 single-arm trials cannot yield a valid pairwise NMA. QUARANTINED band is the correct disposition. Flagging only HIGH-severity item per chunk protocol: review topology is incompatible with NMA — needs comparator-construction step (e.g., synthetic control or proportion meta-analysis) before any pooled effect is computed.

First row in QUARANTINED review has name='NCT02773849' (i.e., the NCT itself was used as trial name). Missing trial name (Nadofaragene firadenovec is the agent; trial commonly 'CS-003' or no acronym). The placeholder name suggests an extractor fallback when no acronym was found — should be human-verified rather than left as NCT-as-name.

log.evidence: PubMed PMID 31539289 is the AMES trial (NCT02968914) — a benralizumab autoinjector-vs-prefilled-syringe pharmacokinetic device comparison in 180 healthy adults. Not GREGALE (which itself is the at-home APFS functionality study, not a CRSwNP efficacy RCT). DOI: 10.1080/02770903.2019.1663428
log.recommended_action: Replace PMID with the actual GREGALE-or-CRSwNP-efficacy citation; flag that GREGALE is historically a benralizumab at-home device study, not CRSwNP — the entire row may be a name collision.

log.claim: pmid=32195649 attached to fosmanogepix REVIVE-1 (rare fungal Lomentospora)
log.evidence: PubMed PMID 32195649 is the iclaprim REVIVE-1/-2 pooled analysis for ABSSSI wound infections (J Med Microbiol 2020). Not fosmanogepix and not antifungal. The 'REVIVE' name belongs to iclaprim antibacterial trials; fosmanogepix Phase-2 mold trial is AEGIS (NCT04240886, already in dataset). DOI: 10.1099/jmm.0.001177
log.recommended_action: Verify NCT04125407 actually exists and matches description. Likely a trial-name confusion: REVIVE in antifungal context should not reuse the iclaprim REVIVE PMID.

log.claim: Fosmanogepix vs caspofungin (candidemia), tE=100/100 cE=95/100, HR 1.16 [0.99, 1.36], pmid=40203286
log.evidence: PubMed PMID 40203286 with DOI 10.1093/cid/ciaf185 confirms NCT04240886 is the AEGIS Phase-2 OPEN-LABEL SINGLE-ARM study of fosmanogepix in invasive mold disease (Aspergillus/rare molds), N=21. It is NOT MIRROR, NOT vs caspofungin, NOT candidemia, and the 100/100 vs 95/100 count structure is fabricated for a single-arm n=21 trial.
log.recommended_action: Correct group label to AEGIS, set cE/cN to 0, single-arm, reset HR to null, and verify the existence of a separate MIRROR fosmanogepix-vs-caspofungin candidemia trial.

log.evidence: PubMed PMID 30891585 is 'Pericoronary adipose tissue attenuation and coronary artery disease' (Eur Heart J Cardiovasc Imaging 2019). Unrelated to lefamulin or CABP. Correct LEAP-1 PMID is 30326510 (JAMA 2019). DOI: 10.1093/ehjci/jez031
log.recommended_action: Replace with correct PMID (30326510 / JAMA 2019 LEAP-1 main paper).

log.claim: pmid=28129986 attached to REGAIN eculizumab AChR-MG
log.evidence: PubMed PMID 28129986 is a Lancet commentary 'Pancreatic cancer: are more chemotherapy and surgery needed?'. Unrelated to myasthenia gravis. Correct REGAIN PMID is 28284985 (Lancet Neurol 2017). DOI: 10.1016/S0140-6736(17)30126-5
PubMed PMID 21366471 is 'Combating environmental causes of cancer' (NEJM Perspective, 2011). DOSE-AHF's correct NEJM 2011 article PMID is 21366472 (off by one). DOI: 10.1056/NEJMp1006634

log.claim: tE=0, cE=0, tN=227, cN=226 yet publishedHR=0.65 [0.52, 0.81], estimandType=null
log.evidence: Both event counts are zero, which mathematically cannot produce a finite HR with these CIs. The HR/CI values are correct for MIRASOL PFS (NEJM 2023, real HR≈0.65), but the event counts are placeholder, and estimandType is null where 'HR' is required.
log.recommended_action: Populate tE/cE from the source paper (PFS events ~ 178 vs 199); set estimandType='HR'.

log.evidence: PEGASUS primary was Hb change from baseline, MD = +3.84 g/dL (95% CI 2.33-5.36) at week 16. Value is the mean difference; field name 'publishedHR' is a misnomer but estimandType='MD' is consistent with the values. Label OK; field-name carries semantic risk.
log.recommended_action: Field-name remains a project-wide design risk: 'publishedHR' is used as a generic effect slot. No data change needed for PEGASUS, but flag for global rename.

log.evidence: EU-PORIA is a single-arm real-world PFA registry. cN=616 is fabricated; cE=0 is implausible if cN were real. HR with CI cannot be derived from a single-arm registry alongside a 100%-event treatment row.
log.recommended_action: Drop control arm or convert to single-arm proportion; remove HR. Pattern repeats for PULSE-AF (NCT04280549).

log.evidence: PULSED-AF (PulseSelect pivotal IDE, Verma et al. Circulation 2023) is a single-arm pivotal study, not vs a control arm. Real freedom-from-AF rates ~66% AF / ~55% AFL, not 150/150. Both rows are implausible: 100% efficacy in treatment and a synthetic control.
log.recommended_action: Convert to single-arm proportion; reset cE/cN to 0; remove HR and CI.

log.claim: tE=0, tN=58, cE=20, cN=47, publishedHR=0.025 [0.005, 0.13], estimandType=HR
log.evidence: With tE=0, the HR can be approximated but the precise value 0.025 cannot be derived. Real reported HR (Pittock 2023, Ann Neurol) was 0.014 (95% CI 0.000-0.103) with attack-adjudicated counts of 0 vs 20. Numeric drift suggests imputation, not source-derived.
log.recommended_action: Replace HR with the source value or re-derive from continuity-corrected events.

log.evidence: Trial size 2000 is plausible for MVA-BN PRO-001-like programs but the exact 100% event-rate vs exact 10% in control with HR/OR=17.5 and tight CI [15.4, 19.9] looks like synthetic round-number placeholder data, not extraction from a real source.
log.recommended_action: Re-extract counts from primary publication; consider QUARANTINING this row.

log.evidence: Schema violation: estimandType is null for a row carrying a usable HR + CI. Downstream meta-analysis engines will skip or misclassify the row.

log.claim: OR=47.5 [2.96, 762] derived from cE=0 (zero events in delayed-treatment arm)
log.evidence: Extreme OR width with UCI=762 is a classic zero-cell artifact. Cochrane handbook flags this as needing continuity correction or alternative effect measure. The HEP_D review is already QUARANTINED — this row is one of the drivers.
log.recommended_action: Per chunk-8 constraints, only HIGH flagged on QUARANTINED. Apply 0.5 continuity correction or report as risk difference.
